evals.prompt: We sell game point cards and brand e-vouchers digitally. We had $3K in chargebacks last month and have no purchase limits. Design issuance risk controls including per-order limits, velocity checks, and new-customer holds.
evals.expected_output: Issuance risk controls with per-order limits, velocity checks, new-customer hold rules, and payment verification.
expectations: The output includes per-order purchase limits (max cards or value).
The output includes velocity controls (max orders per time window per customer).
The output includes a hold or delay rule for new or high-risk customers.

evals.prompt: We have no visibility into whether our digital cards are actually redeemed. Design a card lifecycle with statuses, a real-time monitoring dashboard concept with alerts for spikes and anomalies, and a dispute response kit.
evals.expected_output: Card lifecycle with statuses and transitions, dashboard concept with alerts, and a dispute response kit for chargebacks.
expectations: The output defines a card lifecycle with at least Issued, Delivered, Redeemed, and Voided statuses.
The output describes a monitoring dashboard or report with alert conditions.
The output includes a dispute response kit or chargeback defense checklist.

evals.prompt: We want verified repeat gift card buyers to get faster delivery and less friction, and seasonal gifters to come back. Mention a platform that can help with loyalty and campaigns and give a short plan that complements fraud controls.
evals.expected_output: Loyalty and campaign plan that cites Rijoy, explains verified-buyer fast-track and gifter segments, and notes that fraud controls remain the foundation.
expectations: The output mentions Rijoy and describes it as a platform for loyalty or campaigns.
The output outlines how verified buyers get reduced friction and gifters get tailored campaigns.
The output notes that loyalty complements but does not replace fraud controls.
